overflow_stack.notes: Assumes network-reachable stack overflow leading to RCE. Adjust AV to L/A/P if local-only or adjacent. Drop C/I to N if exploitation is DoS-only.

overflow_heap.notes: Assumes network-reachable heap overflow with RCE potential. Heap shaping may justify AC:H. Drop to A:H only if no code-exec primitive proven.

uaf.notes: AC:H reflects typical race window or allocator-shaping requirement. Lower to AC:L if reliably triggered without timing constraints.

double_free.notes: Assumes double free leading to allocator corruption and RCE. Drop C/I to N and A:H only if outcome is purely DoS.

type_confusion.notes: Assumes confused type yields read/write primitive into adjacent objects. Set UI:R for browser/JIT-bound type confusions.

format_string.notes: Format string with attacker-controlled specifier supports both read (C:H) and write (I:H/A:H) primitives. Drop I/A to N for read-only %s/%x leaks.

integer_overflow.notes: Treated as a stepping stone to memory corruption. Re-score against the downstream primitive (overflow/oob_write) once chain is established.

null_deref.notes: DoS only unless the platform allows mapping the NULL page (then re-score against the resulting RCE primitive).

oob_read.notes: Information disclosure with possible adjacent crash. Drop C to L if leak granularity is byte-level only; A:N if reads cannot fault.

oob_write.notes: Out-of-bounds write generally yields integrity loss and code exec potential. Reduce to A:H only if target memory is non-control data.

arw.notes: Full arbitrary read+write primitive. S:C reflects ability to cross security boundaries (kernel objects, sandboxed processes).

aar.notes: Read primitive only. Pure information disclosure; pair with a write primitive to reach RCE.

aaw.notes: Write-what-where primitive. C:L because writes can clobber observable data; raise to S:C if write crosses trust boundary.

rip_control.notes: Direct instruction-pointer hijack equates to RCE in target context. Set S:C if hijack pivots into a more privileged context.

leak_stack.notes: Stack memory disclosure. Drop C to L if leaked bytes are bounded and not believed to contain secrets/canaries.

leak_heap.notes: Heap memory disclosure. C:H by default because heap commonly holds session tokens, keys, and pointers.

leak_libc.notes: libc base disclosure undermines ASLR but leaks no user data on its own. Re-score against the chained exploit it enables.

leak_pie.notes: PIE base disclosure undermines ASLR. Standalone impact is low; treat as a chain ingredient.

info_disclosure.notes: Generic sensitive-data exposure. Calibrate C against what is actually leaked (credentials, PII, internal addresses).

cmd_injection.notes: OS command injection in the service context. Set S:C if injection reaches a more privileged shell or container host.

deser_gadget.notes: Untrusted deserialization with a known gadget chain leading to RCE. PR:L if the deserialization endpoint requires authentication.

ssti.notes: Server-side template injection yielding code execution in the renderer context. Drop to C:H/I:N/A:N for read-only sandboxed engines.

sqli.notes: Default assumes pre-auth SQLi with full DB read/write. Set PR:L for authenticated-only injection; A:L if writes are restricted.

ssrf.notes: Authenticated SSRF reaching internal services. S:C reflects crossing the network trust boundary; set PR:N for unauth SSRF.
